company_type: Healthcare and Life Science Products and Services

supply_chain.dependencies: Single-sourced materials in certain regions including ethylene oxide (EO) and radioisotope cobalt-60 necessary for AST operations
risks: Limited number of suppliers for key materials such as ethylene oxide and cobalt-60
Supply chain disruptions from geopolitical conflicts
Tariff measures and trade policy changes creating inflation risks
Availability and pricing volatility of raw materials including stainless steel, organic and inorganic chemicals, fuel, and plastic components
Single-source suppliers for certain materials in specific regions

regulatory_risks: FDA regulatory requirements for medical devices and sterilization products
EPA regulations on ethylene oxide (EO) emissions and sterilization operations
State and local regulatory actions related to EO sterilization facilities
European Union regulations including GDPR, EU Customs Code, EU Restriction of Hazardous Substances Directive
US Foreign Corrupt Practices Act and UK Bribery Act compliance
Corporate Sustainability Reporting Directive (CSRD) and European Sustainability Reporting Standards (ESRS)
Global Base Erosion (GloBE) / Pillar Two minimum tax rules
Medical Device Manufacturers Association (MDMA) and AdvaMed Code of Ethics compliance
MedTech Europe Code of Ethical Business Practice
Changes in healthcare reimbursement policies (Medicare, Medicaid)
Environmental, health, and safety regulations across multiple jurisdictions
Tax law changes including Section 7874 of the U.S. Internal Revenue Code
Potential denial of U.S. government contracts to redomiciled companies
Country-specific regulations in over 35 countries where STERIS operates

sanction_risks: Trade restrictions and limitations on trade with sanctioned countries
Tariff measures introduced in early calendar year 2025 creating uncertainty
Potential retaliatory tariffs from other countries
Changes to international trade agreements and policies
